equivalence_argument: No complete mapping: the underlying port ChainSpec supports unbounded extra_data, but nodebuilder Ritual mainnet construction does not set max_extra_data_bytes(None) and RitualChainSpec does not delegate max_extra_data_bytes through EthChainSpec.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: RITUAL_MAINNET and RitualChainSpecBuilder::ritual_mainnet do not explicitly set max_extra_data_bytes(None), and RitualChainSpec does not expose a max_extra_data_bytes EthChainSpec override/delegation in nodebuilder.
related_but_insufficient_code: The nodebuilder chainspec source has no max_extra_data_bytes symbol, while the port ChainSpec API does expose the field.

notes: The likely owner is nodebuilder chainspec, not the base port chainspec field implementation.
rationale: The merge adds max_extra_data_bytes(None) to both the default Ritual mainnet builder and static mainnet spec, and exposes the field via EthChainSpec delegation. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: RITUAL_MAINNET and RitualChainSpecBuilder::ritual_mainnet must set max_extra_data_bytes(None), and RitualChainSpec must delegate max_extra_data_bytes through EthChainSpec.
suggested_falsification_tests: Construct RITUAL_MAINNET and RitualChainSpecBuilder::ritual_mainnet().build(), then assert max_extra_data_bytes() returns None for both and block/header validation accepts extra_data longer than the default Ethereum limit when other rules pass.
summary: Ritual nodebuilder chainspec does not remove the extra-data byte cap.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Construct nodebuilder RITUAL_MAINNET and RitualChainSpecBuilder::ritual_mainnet().build() and assert max_extra_data_bytes() returns None; current source lacks the setter/delegation.
title: Ritual chainspec has no extra-data byte cap
